summary: Matched `melee/ft/ftstarrodswing.c` by adding the missing Star Rod swing action/setup and accessory callback logic, tightening related fighter and item prototypes, and flipping the object from NonMatching to Matching in the build configuration. The PR also corrected the `ftstarrodswing.c` `.sdata2` split end address from `0x804D8F10` to `0x804D8F0C`, indicating the matched C used less or different constant data than the previous split expected.
what_changed: `config/GALE01/splits.txt` adjusted the `.sdata2` range for `melee/ft/ftstarrodswing.c`, changing the end from `0x804D8F10` to `0x804D8F0C`.

`ftstarrodswing.c` added `ftCo_800CD82C`, which initializes Star Rod swing command variables, calls `ftCo_800CD140(gobj, arg1, 0, arg2)`, installs `ft_800CD914` as `accessory4_cb`, and sets `take_dmg_cb` to `ft_800CD31C`.

`ftstarrodswing.c` added `ft_800CD914`, the Star Rod swing accessory callback, including item-kind assertion, Star Rod SFX handling, throw flag consumption, bone/joint position conversion, projectile/effect spawning, and alternate behavior when `it_8026B594` reports a different item state.
Header prototypes were refined from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to typed signatures for `ftCo_800CD140`, `ft_800CD914`, `it_80292394`, and `it_802923BC`.

`ftstarrodswing.c` gained includes for effects, fighter helpers/inlines, Star Rod item APIs, and vector/joint helper functionality: `ef/efasync.h`, `ft/ft_0877.h`, `ft/inlines.h`, `it/items/itstarrod.h`, and `lb/lb_00B0.h`.
smart_moves: Converted dependent unknown prototypes to concrete typed signatures instead of forcing casts or keeping `UNK_PARAMS`, which made the matched implementation cleaner and more reusable.
Used the fighter command variable pattern directly: `cmd_vars[0]` is initialized to sentinel `540000`, later interpreted as a pending SFX id, and reset after playback.
Preserved runtime safety and expected item ownership assumptions with `HSD_ASSERT(100, itGetKind(fp->item_gobj) == It_Kind_StarRod)` before calling Star Rod item helpers.

Separated the two Star Rod states through repeated `it_8026B594(fp->item_gobj)` checks: normal state plays the command SFX/projectile path, while the alternate state uses SFX `234` and effect id `1032` with a zero float parameter.
Consumed `fp->throw_flags_b0` by clearing it and converting it into a local boolean gate before spawning projectile/effects, matching the original control-flow shape.
Used `PAD_STACK(8)`, suggesting stack layout padding was needed for matching rather than just source-level clarity.
decomp_lessons: When matching fighter item-swing files, check nearby item headers first; unknown item helper prototypes often need concrete `Item_GObj*`, `Vec*`, `float`, or `int` signatures to unlock clean C.
Callback setup functions may be short but important for matching: initialize fighter state, call shared action setup helpers, and assign accessory/damage callbacks in the same order as the original.
Sentinel constants in `cmd_vars` can be meaningful matching anchors. Here `540000` marks a no-pending-SFX or consumed-SFX state for the Star Rod swing callback.
Fighter accessory callbacks often combine animation command flags, held-item validation, joint-space position lookup, effect spawning, and item-specific projectile spawning.
After a file becomes matching, revisit split metadata; the matched source can reveal that an old `.sdata2` boundary included too much data.
Prefer typed prototypes over `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` as soon as a caller reveals argument order and return type. This PR demonstrates that small header cleanup can be part of the matching fix.
naming_conventions: Existing named action callbacks use the `ftCo_StarRodSwing_*` pattern: `ftCo_StarRodSwing_Anim`, `ftCo_StarRodSwing_IASA`, `ftCo_StarRodSwing_Phys`, and `ftCo_StarRodSwing_Coll`.
Unidentified or address-based fighter helpers retain address-style names such as `ftCo_800CD82C` and `ft_800CD914`.
Star Rod item helpers retain address-style item names such as `it_80292394`, `it_802923BC`, and `it_8026B594` when their semantic names are not established.
Typed object aliases are used in prototypes: `Fighter_GObj*` for fighter game objects and `Item_GObj*` for item game objects.
The item kind enum name observed in the assertion is `It_Kind_StarRod`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted `PAD_STACK(8)` in `ft_800CD914`, likely to reproduce the original stack frame layout.
Declared locals in a shape consistent with matching needs: integer temporaries `x` and `y`, two `Vec` locals, and a `float f` for the alternate effect spawn path.
Used `false` in one comparison to `it_8026B594` and `0` in another, preserving source forms that may influence generated code.
Kept explicit repeated `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` use inside `efAsync_Spawn` arguments instead of always reusing `fp`, which may have helped match instruction selection or register allocation.
Adjusted `.sdata2` end address by four bytes, from `0x804D8F10` to `0x804D8F0C`, after producing matching code.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The context reports one issue comment, but its body was not included in the excerpt, so no substantive review feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type `it_80292394`, `it_802923BC`, or `it_8026B594`.
Look for PRs matching `ftlipstickswing.c`, since it is adjacent in the splits and configure list.
Search for other fighter item swing callbacks using `cmd_vars[0] = 540000` to confirm the sentinel convention.
Search for uses of `throw_flags_b0` in item-swing or weapon-spawn callbacks to compare matching patterns.
Check whether `ftCo_800CD82C` later receives a semantic name tied to Star Rod swing action setup.
